new_classes.label_en: Wine of fresh grapes, incl. fortified wines; grape must (heading 2204)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben, einschl. mit Alkohol angereicherter Wein; Traubenmost (Position 2204)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, including fortified wines, and grape must other than that of heading 2009, as defined under CN heading 2204. Includes still and sparkling wines, as well as partly fermented grape must with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding 0.5% vol.

Grape must classified under CN heading 2204, being partly fermented grape must or grape must whose fermentation has been arrested other than by the addition of alcohol, explicitly excluding grape must of heading 2009 (unfermented juice).
Wine of fresh grapes to which alcohol has been added to arrest fermentation or to raise the alcoholic strength, classified under CN heading 2204.

A quality of a beverage expressing the ratio of the volume of alcohol actually present to the total volume of the product, expressed as a percentage by volume (% vol), as referred to in Additional Note 2(a) to Chapter 22 of the CN.

coverage_explanation: The proposed axioms cover the three main product types under heading 2204: (1) wine of fresh grapes, (2) fortified wines, and (3) grape must other than heading 2009. The ActualAlcoholicStrengthByVolume quality class captures the reference to Additional Note 2(a) on 'actual alcoholic strength by volume'. Coverage is slightly reduced (0.85) because: (a) the precise numeric threshold for 'actual alcoholic strength' referenced in Additional Note 2(a) is not encoded here (that note is not provided in full), and (b) the exclusion of grape must of heading 2009 is expressed only via a complement restriction on the grape must class, which is a pragmatic approximation rather than a full cross-heading disjointness axiom. No regulatory measure individuals (footnotes, certificates) are present in the TBox for this heading, so none are referenced.
